name: Quaker's: Vanishing Oatmeal Raisin Cookies
recipeIngredient: 3 cups QUAKER Oats uncooked (quick or old fashioned)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cream together the margarine and sugars until smooth.
3. Add eggs and vanilla; beat well.
4. Stir in the combined flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt; mix well.
5. Fold in the oats and raisins; mix well.
6. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough onto an ungreased cookie sheet.
7. Bake 10 to 12 minutes or until light golden brown.
8. Allow cookies to cool for 1 minute on the cookie sheet, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
Easy Bar Cookies:
1. Press cooled dough evenly into an ungreased 13x9-inch baking pan.
2. Bake at 350°F for 30 to 35 minutes or until lightly golden brown.
3. Allow bars to cool completely before cutting and storing tightly covered (32 BARS).
High Altitude Adjustment:
1. Increase the flour to 2 cups.
2. Bake as directed.
Ice Cream Sandwich Cookies:
1. Place one softened cookie on a plate.
2. Top with another cookie.
3. Wrap in plastic wrap or aluminum foil and freeze.
4. Allow cookies to thaw slightly before serving.
Chocolate Dipped Cookies:
1. Melt semisweet chocolate pieces in the microwave (HIGH for 1-2 minutes, stirring every 20 seconds) or over a double boiler until smooth.
2. Dip half of the cooled cookies into the melted chocolate.
3. Tap off excess chocolate and sprinkle with finely chopped nuts if desired.
4. Place on wax paper to set. If the chocolate thickens, reheat in short intervals or allow it to warm again until fluid before serving.
